(**BEng** or **BE**) or a **Bachelor of Science in Engineering** (**BSE[[1]](#cite_note-1)**) is an [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate") [academic degree](/wiki/Academic_degree "Academic degree") awarded to a college graduate majoring in an engineering discipline at a higher education institution.

(Redirected from [B.E.](/w/index.php?title=B.E.&redirect=no "B.E."))

Type of undergraduate qualification

A **Bachelor of Engineering** (**BEng** or **BE**) or a **Bachelor of Science in Engineering** (**BSE[[1]](#cite_note-1)**) is an [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate") [academic degree](/wiki/Academic_degree "Academic degree") awarded to a college graduate majoring in an engineering discipline at a higher education institution.

In the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), a Bachelor of Engineering degree program is accredited by one of the [Engineering Council](/wiki/Engineering_Council "Engineering Council")'s professional engineering institutions as suitable for registration as an [incorporated engineer](/wiki/Incorporated_engineer "Incorporated engineer") or [chartered engineer](/wiki/Chartered_engineer "Chartered engineer") with further study to masters level. In Canada, a degree from a Canadian university can be accredited by the [Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board](/wiki/Canadian_Engineering_Accreditation_Board "Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board") (CEAB). Alternatively, it might be accredited directly by another professional engineering institution, such as the US-based [Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers](/wiki/Institute_of_Electrical_and_Electronics_Engineers "Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers") (IEEE). The Bachelor of Engineering contributes to the route to [chartered engineer (UK)](/wiki/Chartered_engineer_(UK) "Chartered engineer (UK)"), registered engineer or licensed [professional engineer](/wiki/Professional_engineer "Professional engineer") and has been approved by representatives of the profession. Similarly Bachelor of Engineering (BE) and Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in India is accredited by [All India Council for Technical Education](/wiki/All_India_Council_for_Technical_Education "All India Council for Technical Education"). Most universities in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") and [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") award bachelor's degrees in engineering through various names.

A less common and possibly the oldest variety of the degree in the English-speaking world is *Baccalaureus in Arte Ingeniaria* (B.A.I.), a [Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin") name meaning *Bachelor in the Art of Engineering*.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Here *Baccalaureus in Arte Ingeniaria* implies excellence in carrying out the 'art' or 'function' of an engineer. Some South African universities refer to their engineering degrees as B.Ing. (**Baccalaureus Ingenieurswese**, in [Afrikaans](/wiki/Afrikaans "Afrikaans")).

## Engineering fields

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Engineering&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Engineering fields")]

A Bachelor of Engineering degree will usually be undertaken in one field of engineering, which is sometimes noted in the degree [postnominals](/wiki/Postnominals "Postnominals"), as in *B.E.*, *B.AE. (Aero)*, or *B.Eng (Elec)*. Common fields for the Bachelor of Engineering degree include the following fields:

* [Aerospace Engineering](/wiki/Aerospace_Engineering "Aerospace Engineering")
* [Agricultural Engineering](/wiki/Agricultural_Engineering "Agricultural Engineering")
* [Architectural Engineering](/wiki/Architectural_Engineering "Architectural Engineering")
* [Automotive Engineering](/wiki/Automotive_Engineering "Automotive Engineering")
* [Biological Engineering](/wiki/Biological_Engineering "Biological Engineering") — including [Biochemical](/wiki/Biochemical_engineering "Biochemical engineering"), [Biomedical](/wiki/Biomedical_engineering "Biomedical engineering"), Biosystems and [Biomolecular](/wiki/Biomolecular_engineering "Biomolecular engineering")
* [Chemical Engineering](/wiki/Chemical_Engineering "Chemical Engineering") — deals with the process of converting raw materials or chemicals into more useful or valuable forms
* [Civil Engineering](/wiki/Civil_Engineering "Civil Engineering") — a wide-ranging field, including [building engineering](/wiki/Building_engineering "Building engineering"), civil engineering, construction engineering, industrial, [manufacturing](/wiki/Manufacturing "Manufacturing"), mechanical, materials and control engineering
* [Clean Technology](/wiki/Clean_Technology "Clean Technology") — use energy, water and raw materials and other inputs more efficiently and productively. Create less waste or toxicity and deliver equal or superior performance.
* [Computer Engineering](/wiki/Computer_Engineering "Computer Engineering")
* [Computer Science and Engineering](/wiki/Computer_Science_and_Engineering "Computer Science and Engineering")
* [Control Engineering](/wiki/Control_Engineering "Control Engineering") — a relatively new and more specialized [subfield](/wiki/Electrical_Engineering#Subfields "Electrical Engineering") of Electrical Engineering that focuses on integrating Electrical Controls and their programming.
* [Electrical and Computer Engineering](/wiki/Electrical_and_Computer_Engineering "Electrical and Computer Engineering")/[Electronic Engineering](/wiki/Electronic_Engineering "Electronic Engineering") — very diverse field, including Computer Engineering, [Communication/Communication systems engineering](/wiki/Telecommunications_engineering "Telecommunications engineering"), [Information Technology](/wiki/Information_Technology "Information Technology"), Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, [Microelectronic Engineering](/wiki/Microelectronic_Engineering "Microelectronic Engineering"), Microelectronics, [Nanotechnology](/wiki/Nanotechnology "Nanotechnology"), Mechatronics, Software Engineering, Systems, [Wireless](/wiki/Wireless "Wireless") and [Telecommunications](/wiki/Telecommunications "Telecommunications"), [Photovoltaic](/wiki/Photovoltaic "Photovoltaic") and [Power Engineering](/wiki/Power_Engineering "Power Engineering")
* [Engineering Management](/wiki/Engineering_Management "Engineering Management") — the application of engineering principles to the planning and operational management of industrial and manufacturing operations
* [Environmental Engineering](/wiki/Environmental_Engineering "Environmental Engineering") — includes fields such as Environmental, Geological, Geomatic, Mining, Marine and [Ocean Engineering](/wiki/Ocean_Engineering "Ocean Engineering")
* [Fire Protection Engineering](/wiki/Fire_Protection_Engineering "Fire Protection Engineering") — the application of science and engineering principles to protect people and their environments from the destructive effects of fire and smoke.
* [Geological Engineering](/wiki/Geological_Engineering "Geological Engineering") — a hybrid discipline that comprises elements of civil engineering, mining engineering, petroleum engineering and [earth sciences](/wiki/Earth_sciences "Earth sciences").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* [Geomatics Engineering](/wiki/Geomatics_Engineering "Geomatics Engineering") — acquisition, modeling analysis and management of spatial data. Focuses on satellite positioning, [remote sensing](/wiki/Remote_sensing "Remote sensing"), land surveying, wireless location and [Geographic Information Systems](/wiki/Geographic_Information_Systems "Geographic Information Systems") (GIS).[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Geotechnical Engineering](/wiki/Geotechnical_Engineering "Geotechnical Engineering") — a combination of civil and mining engineering and involves the analysis of [earth materials](/wiki/Earth_materials "Earth materials").
* [Industrial Engineering](/wiki/Industrial_Engineering "Industrial Engineering") — studies facilities planning, plant layout, work measurement, job design, methods engineering, human factors, manufacturing processes, [operations management](/wiki/Operations_management "Operations management"), statistical quality control, systems, psychology and basic operations management
* [Information Engineering](/wiki/Information_Engineering "Information Engineering") — same as Information Technology.
* [Instrumentation Engineering](/wiki/Instrumentation_Engineering "Instrumentation Engineering") — a branch of engineering dealing with measurement
* [Integrated Engineering](/wiki/Integrated_Engineering "Integrated Engineering") — a multi-disciplinary, design-project-based engineering degree program.
* [Leather](/wiki/Leather "Leather") Engineering — an applied chemistry type based on leather and its application.
* [Manufacturing Engineering](/wiki/Manufacturing_Engineering "Manufacturing Engineering"): Includes methods engineering, manufacturing process planning, tool design, metrology, Robotics, [Computer integrated manufacturing](/wiki/Computer_integrated_manufacturing "Computer integrated manufacturing"), [operations management](/wiki/Operations_management "Operations management") and [manufacturing management](/wiki/Manufacturing_process_management "Manufacturing process management")
* [Marine Engineering](/wiki/Marine_Engineering "Marine Engineering") — includes the engineering of [boats, ships, oil rigs and any other marine vessel](/wiki/Watercraft "Watercraft") or structure, as well as oceanographic engineering. Specifically, marine engineering is the discipline of applying engineering sciences, including mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, electronic engineering and [computer science](/wiki/Computer_science "Computer science"), to the development, design, operation and maintenance of [watercraft propulsion](/wiki/Marine_propulsion "Marine propulsion") and on-board systems and oceanographic technology. It includes but is not limited to power and propulsion plants, [machinery](/wiki/Machinery "Machinery"), [piping](/wiki/Piping "Piping"), [automation](/wiki/Automation "Automation") and [control systems](/wiki/Control_systems "Control systems") for marine vehicles of any kind, such as surface ships and submarines.
* [Materials Engineering](/wiki/Materials_Engineering "Materials Engineering") — includes metallurgy, [polymer](/wiki/Polymer_engineering "Polymer engineering") and [ceramic engineering](/wiki/Ceramic_engineering "Ceramic engineering")
* [Mechanical Engineering](/wiki/Mechanical_Engineering "Mechanical Engineering") — includes engineering of total systems where mechanical science principles apply to objects in motion including [transportation](/wiki/Transportation "Transportation"), [energy](/wiki/Energy "Energy"), [buildings](/wiki/Buildings "Buildings"), [aerospace](/wiki/Aerospace "Aerospace") and [machine design](/wiki/Machine_design "Machine design"). Explores the applications of the theoretical fields of [Mechanics](/wiki/Mechanics "Mechanics"), [kinematics](/wiki/Kinematics "Kinematics"), [thermodynamics](/wiki/Thermodynamics "Thermodynamics"), materials science, [structural analysis](/wiki/Structural_analysis "Structural analysis"), manufacturing and [electricity](/wiki/Electricity "Electricity")
* [Mechatronics Engineering](/wiki/Mechatronics_Engineering "Mechatronics Engineering") - includes a combination of mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, telecommunications engineering, control engineering and computer engineering
* [Mining Engineering](/wiki/Mining_Engineering "Mining Engineering") — deals with discovering, extracting, beneficiating, marketing and utilizing mineral deposits.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Nuclear Engineering](/wiki/Nuclear_Engineering "Nuclear Engineering") — customarily includes [nuclear fission](/wiki/Nuclear_fission "Nuclear fission"), [nuclear fusion](/wiki/Nuclear_fusion "Nuclear fusion") and related topics such as heat/thermodynamics transport, nuclear fuel or other related technology (*e.g.*, radioactive waste disposal) and the problems of nuclear proliferation. May also include [radiation protection](/wiki/Radiation_protection "Radiation protection"), [particle detectors](/wiki/Particle_detector "Particle detector") and [medical physics](/wiki/Medical_physics "Medical physics").
* [Petroleum Engineering](/wiki/Petroleum_Engineering "Petroleum Engineering") — a field of engineering concerned with the activities related to [exploration](/wiki/Hydrocarbon_exploration "Hydrocarbon exploration") and production of [hydrocarbons](/wiki/Hydrocarbons "Hydrocarbons") from the Earth's subsurface.
* [Plastics Engineering](/wiki/Plastics_Engineering "Plastics Engineering") — A vast field which includes plastic processing, mold designing...
* [Process Engineering](/wiki/Process_Engineering "Process Engineering") — the understanding and application of the fundamental principles and laws of nature that allow humans to transform raw material and energy into products that are useful to society, at an industrial level.
* [Production Engineering](/wiki/Production_Engineering "Production Engineering") — a term used in the UK and Europe similar to Industrial Engineering in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"). It includes the engineering of machines, people, processes and management. Explores the applications of the theoretical field of Mechanics.
* [Robotics](/wiki/Robotics "Robotics") and [Automation Engineering](/wiki/Automation_engineering "Automation engineering")  — relates all engineering fields for implementation in robotics and automation
* [Software Engineering](/wiki/Software_Engineering "Software Engineering") — systematic application of scientific and technological knowledge, methods and experience to the design, implementation, testing and documentation of software
* [Structural Engineering](/wiki/Structural_Engineering "Structural Engineering") — analyze, design, plan and research structural components, systems and loads, in order to achieve design goals including high-risk structures ensuring the safety and comfort of users or occupants in a wide range of specialties.
* [Systems Engineering](/wiki/Systems_Engineering "Systems Engineering") — focuses on the analysis, design, development and organization of complex systems
* [Textile Engineering](/wiki/Textile_Engineering "Textile Engineering") — based on the conversion of three types of [fiber](/wiki/Fiber "Fiber") into [yarn](/wiki/Yarn "Yarn"), then [fabric](/wiki/Fabric "Fabric"), then [textiles](/wiki/Textiles "Textiles")[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-:0-10)

## International variations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Engineering&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International variations")]

### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Engineering&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Australia")]

In [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), the *Bachelor of Engineering* (BE(Hons) or BEng(Hons) - depending on the institution) is a four-year undergraduate professional degree course with an embedded Honours year, at Level 8 in the [Australian Qualifications Framework (AQF)](/wiki/Australian_Qualifications_Framework "Australian Qualifications Framework").[[10]](#cite_note-:0-10) It is described by the Australian Council of Engineering Deans (ACED) as delivering "more advanced, professional and research-based outcomes than any Bachelor Degree."[[10]](#cite_note-:0-10) Some institutions in Australia also provide three-year *Bachelor of Engineering Technology* degrees at Level 7 of the AQF that qualify their graduates to start practice as Engineering Technologists.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The title of “engineer” is not protected in Australia, therefore anyone can claim to be an engineer and practice without the necessary competencies, understanding of standards or in compliance with a code of ethics.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The industry has attempted to overcome the lack of title protection through chartership (CPEng), national registration (NER) and various state registration (RPEQ) programs which are usually obtained after a few years of professional practice.

### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Engineering&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Canada")]

In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), degrees awarded for undergraduate engineering studies include the *Bachelor of Engineering* (B.Eng. or B.E., depending on the institution); the **Baccalauréat en génie** (B.Ing., the French equivalent of a B.Eng.; sometimes referred to as a *Baccalauréat en ingénierie*); the *Bachelor of Applied Science* (B.A.Sc.); and the *Bachelor of Science in Engineering* (B.Sc.Eng.).

The [Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board](/wiki/Canadian_Engineering_Accreditation_Board "Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board") (CEAB), a division of the [Engineers Canada](/wiki/Engineers_Canada "Engineers Canada"), sets out and maintains the standards of accreditation among Canadian undergraduate engineering programs. Graduates of those programs are deemed by the profession to have the required academic qualifications to be licensed as professional engineers in Canada.[[13]](#cite_note-13) This practice is intended to maintain standards of education and allow mobility of engineers in different provinces of Canada.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

A CEAB-accredited degree is the minimum academic requirement for registration as a professional engineer anywhere in the country and the standard against which all other engineering academic qualifications are measured.[[15]](#cite_note-apegm1-15) Graduation from an accredited program, which normally involves four years of study, is a required first step to becoming a professional engineer. Regulation and accreditation are accomplished through a self-governing body (the name of which varies from province to province), which is given the power by statute to register and discipline engineers, as well as regulate the field of engineering in the individual provinces.

Graduates of non-CEAB-accredited programs must demonstrate that their education is at least equivalent to that of a graduate of a CEAB-accredited program.[[15]](#cite_note-apegm1-15)

### Nigeria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bachelor_of_Engineering&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Nigeria")]

In [Nigeria](/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria"), the Bachelor of Engineering (B.Eng) is a five year undergraduate professional degree course.

In Nigeria, *Engineer* or Engr is [protected](/wiki/Protected_occupational_title "Protected occupational title"): only a certified engineer may use the title.

Certification comes with registration and accreditation by **[Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria](/wiki/Council_for_the_Regulation_of_Engineering_in_Nigeria "Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria") (COREN)**. It is the regulatory body that governs the practice of engineering in Nigeria.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

The membership is required to practice engineering independently. It is a requirement for some engineering firms and is mandatory for government contracts.
